revision.text:
The '''Yato-no-kami''' (夜刀の神, 'gods of the night sword') are snake deities appearing in the [[Hitachi No Kuni Fudoki]]. They lived in [[Namegata]] county, in fields near the government office. They were rumored to bring familial extermination on anyone who saw them, but were eventually killed by a man clearing the fields, enshrined, and banished to the mountains.
==References== [http://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/cpjr/kami/ito.html] [http://www1.atwiki.jp/occultfantasy/pages/76.html]
{{Japan-myth-stub}}
[[Category:Japanese legendary creatures]]

The '''Yato-no-kami''' (夜刀の神, 'gods of the night sword') are snake deities appearing in the [[Hitachi No Kuni Fudoki]]. They lived in [[Namegata]] county, in fields near the government office. They were rumored to bring familial extermination on anyone who saw them, but were eventually killed by a man clearing the fields, enshrined, and banished to the mountains.
==References== [http://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/cpjr/kami/ito.html] [http://www1.atwiki.jp/occultfantasy/pages/76.html]
{{Japan-myth-stub}}

The {{nihongo|'''Yato-no-kami'''|夜刀の神||"gods of the night-sword"}} are snake deities appearing in the ''[[Hitachi No Kuni Fudoki]]''. They lived in [[Namegata]] county, in fields near the government office. They were rumored to bring familial extermination on anyone who saw them, but were eventually killed by a man clearing the fields, enshrined, and banished to the mountains.
==References== [http://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/cpjr/kami/ito.html] [http://www1.atwiki.jp/occultfantasy/pages/76.html]
{{Japan-myth-stub}}
[[Category:Japanese legendary creatures]] [[ja:夜刀神]]

The {{nihongo|'''Yato-no-kami'''|夜刀の神|Yato-no-kami|"gods of the night-sword"}} are snake deities appearing in the ''[[Hitachi No Kuni Fudoki]]''. They lived in [[Namegata]] county, in fields near the government office. They were rumored to bring familial extermination on anyone who saw them, but were eventually killed by a man clearing the fields, enshrined, and banished to the mountains.
The manga ''[[Blood Sucker (manga)| Blood Sucker: Legend of Zipangu]], originally named ''Yato-no-kami tsukai'', makes them into vampire-like creatures.
==References== [http://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/cpjr/kami/ito.html] [http://www1.atwiki.jp/occultfantasy/pages/76.html]
{{Japan-myth-stub}}
[[Category:Japanese legendary creatures]] [[ja:夜刀神]]

revision.text:
{{Refimprove|date=November 2011}} [[File:Normal Yatogami.png|thumb]] The {{nihongo|'''Yato-no-kami'''|夜刀の神||"gods of the night-sword"}} are snake [[deity|deities]] in Japanese folklore appearing in the ''[[Hitachi No Kuni Fudoki]]''. They lived in [[Namegata]] county, in fields near the government office. They were rumored to bring familial extermination on anyone who saw them. Eventually, they were killed by a man clearing the fields, enshrined, and banished to the mountains. It's also Corrin's Sword.
==References== *[http://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/cpjr/kami/ito.html Evolution of the Concept of Kami] *[http://www1.atwiki.jp/occultfantasy/pages/76.html 夜刀の神(ヤトノカミ・ヤツノカミ)] at [[ATWiki]].jp *The Yato blade is taking on a form I've never seen!
[[Category:Japanese legendary creatures]] [[Category:Legendary serpents]]
{{Japan-myth-stub}}

revision.comment: Undid revision 719228809 by [[Special:Contributions/Sevenrunns|Sevenrunns]] ([[User talk:Sevenrunns|talk]]) Get your terrible game out of here!
I just added more to this from the same source the original writer used.
Edited the order of information to be better and fixed some grammar.
Again found a fault in my grammar so I fixed it.
Sorry. I fixed a typo I made.

It seems I didn't write enough myself last time so this time I wrote everything from my head from what I remember from the Evolution of the concept of kami and shortened it a bit too. I only copied the names like Matachi's. I hope this is better and sorry for the last one.
revision.text:
{{Refimprove|date=November 2011}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yato-no-kami'''|夜刀の神||"gods of the night-sword"}} are snake [[deity|deities]] in Japanese folklore appearing in the ''[[Fudoki|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki]]''. They lived in [[Namegata]] county, in fields near the government office. As a snake, it was an {{nihongo|araburu-kami|荒ぶる神||"malevolent-kami"}} which are a malignant [[kami]] who bring affliction to human beings.<ref>http://www.stonedragonpress.com/minzoku_neo_shinto/topic_004_04_03_02.html</ref> Yato no kami were rumored to bring familial extermination on anyone who saw them.
It's told that Yato no kami were killed and enshrined by a man named Yahazu no uji no Matachi during the time when Emperor [[Keitai]] was in reign. The Yato no kami vanished later when a man named Mibunomuroji Maro drove them away from disturbing him and his workers who were building a mote there.<ref>http://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/cpjr/kami/ito.html</ref>
==References== <references />
==External links== *[http://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/cpjr/kami/ito.html Evolution of the Concept of Kami] *[http://www1.atwiki.jp/occultfantasy/pages/76.html 夜刀の神(ヤトノカミ・ヤツノカミ)] at [[ATWiki]].jp * http://www.stonedragonpress.com/minzoku_neo_shinto/topic_004_04_03_02.html
[[Category:Japanese legendary creatures]] [[Category:Legendary serpents]]
{{Japan-myth-stub}}

revision.text:
{{short description|Snake deities in Japanese folklore}} {{Refimprove|date=November 2011}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yato-no-kami'''|夜刀の神||"gods of the night-sword"}} are snake [[deity|deities]] in Japanese folklore appearing in the ''[[Fudoki|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki]]''. They lived in [[Namegata]] county, in fields near the government office. As a snake, it was an {{nihongo|araburu-kami|荒ぶる神||"malevolent-kami"}} which are a malignant [[kami]] who bring affliction to human beings.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.stonedragonpress.com/minzoku_neo_shinto/topic_004_04_03_02.html|title=民俗ネオ神道 – minzoku NEO-shintô – araburu-kami|website=www.stonedragonpress.com}}</ref> Yato no kami were rumored to bring familial extermination on anyone who saw them.
It's told that Yato no kami were killed and enshrined by a man named Yahazu no uji no Matachi during the time when Emperor [[Keitai]] was in reign. The Yato no kami vanished later when a man named Mibunomuroji Maro drove them away from disturbing him and his workers who were building a moat there.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/cpjr/kami/ito.html|title=Evolution of the Concept of Kami|website=www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>
== In popular culture == In the 2020 action role-playing game [[Nioh 2]], Yato-no-Kami (named Yatsu-no-Kami in-game) makes an appearance as a [[Boss (video games)|boss]] enemy that protagonist has to fight in order to progress. In the game, it takes the form of a giant snake, with 2 smaller snakes protruding from it's body. <ref>{{Cite web|title=Yatsu-no-Kami - Nioh 2|url=https://nioh2.wiki.fextralife.com/Yatsu-no-Kami|access-date=2021-07-26|website=Nioh 2 Wiki|language=en-us}}</ref>
==References== <references />
==External links== *[http://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/cpjr/kami/ito.html Evolution of the Concept of Kami] *[http://www1.atwiki.jp/occultfantasy/pages/76.html 夜刀の神(ヤトノカミ・ヤツノカミ)] at [[ATWiki]].jp * [http://www.stonedragonpress.com/minzoku_neo_shinto/topic_004_04_03_02.html 民俗ネオ神道 – minzoku NEO-shintô – araburu-kami]
[[Category:Japanese legendary creatures]] [[Category:Legendary serpents]]
{{Japan-myth-stub}}

In the game, it takes the form of a giant snake, with 2 smaller snakes protruding from it's body.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Yatsu-no-Kami - Nioh 2|url=https://nioh2.wiki.fextralife.com/Yatsu-no-Kami|access-date=2021-07-26|website=Nioh 2 Wiki|language=en-us}}</ref> In June 2015's Fire Emblem If, the protagonist wields the sword Yato, named after Yato-no-kami. It is their main weapon after chapter 5 of the story.

{{short description|Snake deities in Japanese folklore}} {{Refimprove|date=November 2011}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yato-no-kami'''|夜刀の神||"gods of the night-sword"}} are snake [[deity|deities]] in Japanese folklore appearing in the ''[[Fudoki|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki]]''. They lived in [[Namegata]] county, in fields near the government office. As a snake, it was an {{nihongo|araburu-kami|荒ぶる神||"malevolent-kami"}} which are a malignant [[kami]] who bring affliction to human beings.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.stonedragonpress.com/minzoku_neo_shinto/topic_004_04_03_02.html|title=民俗ネオ神道 – minzoku NEO-shintô – araburu-kami|website=www.stonedragonpress.com}}</ref> Yato no kami were rumored to bring familial extermination on anyone who saw them.
It's told that Yato no kami were killed and enshrined by a man named Yahazu no uji no Matachi during the time when Emperor [[Keitai]] was in reign. The Yato no kami vanished later when a man named Mibunomuroji Maro drove them away from disturbing him and his workers who were building a moat there.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/cpjr/kami/ito.html|title=Evolution of the Concept of Kami|website=www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>
== In popular culture == In the 2020 action role-playing game [[Nioh 2]], Yato-no-Kami (named Yatsu-no-Kami in-game) makes an appearance as a [[Boss (video games)|boss]] enemy that protagonist has to fight in order to progress. In the game, it takes the form of a giant snake, with 2 smaller snakes protruding from it's body.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Yatsu-no-Kami - Nioh 2|url=https://nioh2.wiki.fextralife.com/Yatsu-no-Kami|access-date=2021-07-26|website=Nioh 2 Wiki|language=en-us}}</ref> In June 2015's,<ref>Fire Emblem If</ref> the protagonist wields the sword Yato, named after Yato-no-kami. It is their main weapon after chapter 5 of the story.
==References== <references />https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fire_Emblem_Fates
==External links== *[http://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/cpjr/kami/ito.html Evolution of the Concept of Kami] *[http://www1.atwiki.jp/occultfantasy/pages/76.html 夜刀の神(ヤトノカミ・ヤツノカミ)] *[http://www.stonedragonpress.com/minzoku_neo_shinto/topic_004_04_03_02.html 民俗ネオ神道 – minzoku NEO-shintô – araburu-kami]

== In popular culture == Yato-no-kami appeared as a major villain in [[GeGeGe no Kitarō]]: Nippon Bakuretsu!! in 2008.
In [[Noragami]], Yato is the titular stray god of the series (Noragami) who wants to build his own shrine.
In the 2020 action role-playing game [[Nioh 2]], Yato-no-Kami (named Yatsu-no-Kami in-game) makes an appearance as a [[Boss (video games)|boss]] enemy that protagonist has to fight in order to progress. In the game, it takes the form of a giant snake, with 2 smaller snakes protruding from its body.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Yatsu-no-Kami - Nioh 2|url=https://nioh2.wiki.fextralife.com/Yatsu-no-Kami|access-date=2021-07-26|website=Nioh 2 Wiki|language=en-us}}</ref>
In June 2015's [[Fire Emblem Fates]],<ref>Fire Emblem If</ref> the protagonist wields the sword Yato, named after Yato-no-kami. It is their main weapon after Chapter 5 of the story.

*In [[Noragami]], Yato is the titular stray god of the series (Noragami) who wants to build his own shrine.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://blog.funimation.com/2017/02/20/noragami-aragoto-gods-explained-anime/ | title=The Gods of Noragami Aragoto, Explained | date=20 February 2017 }}</ref>

revision.comment: Reverting edits; in Chapter 25 of the source material, it is shown that Yato-no-kami & the protagonist Yato are two separate individuals.
revision.text:
{{short description|Snake deities in Japanese folklore}} {{Refimprove|date=November 2011}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yato-no-kami'''|夜刀の神||"gods of the night-sword"}} are snake [[deity|deities]] in Japanese folklore appearing in the ''[[Fudoki|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki]]''. They lived in [[Namegata]] county, in fields near the government office. As a snake, it was an {{nihongo|araburu-kami|荒ぶる神||"malevolent-kami"}} which are a malignant [[kami]] who bring affliction to human beings.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.stonedragonpress.com/minzoku_neo_shinto/topic_004_04_03_02.html|title=民俗ネオ神道 – minzoku NEO-shintô – araburu-kami|website=www.stonedragonpress.com}}</ref> Yato no kami were rumored to bring familial extermination on anyone who saw them.
It's told that Yato no kami were killed and enshrined by a man named Yahazu no uji no Matachi during the time when [[Emperor Keitai]] was in reign. The Yato no kami vanished later when a man named Mibunomuroji Maro drove them away from disturbing him and his workers who were building a moat there.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/cpjr/kami/ito.html|title=Evolution of the Concept of Kami|website=www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>
== In popular culture == *Yato-no-kami appeared as a major villain in [[GeGeGe no Kitarō]]: Nippon Bakuretsu!! in 2008.
*In the 2020 action role-playing game [[Nioh 2]], Yato-no-Kami (named Yatsu-no-Kami in-game) makes an appearance as a [[Boss (video games)|boss]] enemy that protagonist has to fight in order to progress. In the game, it takes the form of a giant snake, with 2 smaller snakes protruding from its body.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Yatsu-no-Kami - Nioh 2|url=https://nioh2.wiki.fextralife.com/Yatsu-no-Kami|access-date=2021-07-26|website=Nioh 2 Wiki|language=en-us}}</ref>
*In June 2015's [[Fire Emblem Fates]],<ref>{{Cite web|title=Yato|url=https://fireemblem.fandom.com/wiki/Yato|access-date=2023-05-08|website=Fire Emblem Wiki|language=en-us}}</ref> the protagonist wields the sword Yato, named after Yato-no-kami. It is their main weapon after Chapter 5 of the story.
*In [[Noragami]], Yato-no-kami appears briefly when their name is confused for that of the series protagonist, Yatogami.<ref>Adachitoka. ''The Ties That Bind''. New York, NY: Kodansha USA Publishing, LLC, 2015. Print. Vol. 7 of ''Noragami: Stray God''.</ref>

[[Category:Japanese legendary creatures]] [[Category:Legendary serpents]]
{{Japan-myth-stub}}

{{short description|Snake deities in Japanese folklore}} {{Refimprove|date=November 2011}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yato-no-kami'''|夜刀の神||"gods of the night-sword"}} are snake [[deity|deities]] in Japanese folklore appearing in the ''[[Fudoki|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki]]''. They lived in [[Namegata]] county, in fields near the government office. As a snake, it was an {{nihongo|araburu-kami|荒ぶる神||"malevolent-kami"}} which are a malignant [[kami]] who bring affliction to human beings.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.stonedragonpress.com/minzoku_neo_shinto/topic_004_04_03_02.html|title=民俗ネオ神道 – minzoku NEO-shintô – araburu-kami|website=www.stonedragonpress.com}}</ref> Yato no kami were rumored to bring familial extermination on anyone who saw them.
It's told that Yato no kami were killed and enshrined by a man named Yahazu no uji no Matachi during the time when [[Emperor Keitai]] was in reign. The Yato no kami vanished later when a man named Mibunomuroji Maro drove them away from disturbing him and his workers who were building a moat there.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/cpjr/kami/ito.html|title=Evolution of the Concept of Kami|website=www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>
== In popular culture == *Yato-no-kami appeared as a major villain in [[GeGeGe no Kitarō]]: Nippon Bakuretsu!! in 2008.
*In the 2020 action role-playing game [[Nioh 2]], Yato-no-Kami (named Yatsu-no-Kami in-game) makes an appearance as a [[Boss (video games)|boss]] enemy that protagonist has to fight in order to progress. In the game, it takes the form of a giant snake, with 2 smaller snakes protruding from its body.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Yatsu-no-Kami - Nioh 2|url=https://nioh2.wiki.fextralife.com/Yatsu-no-Kami|access-date=2021-07-26|website=Nioh 2 Wiki|language=en-us}}</ref>
*In June 2015's [[Fire Emblem Fates]],<ref>{{Cite web|title=Yato|url=https://fireemblem.fandom.com/wiki/Yato|access-date=2023-05-08|website=Fire Emblem Wiki|language=en-us}}</ref> the protagonist wields the sword Yato, named after Yato-no-kami. It is their main weapon after Chapter 5 of the story.
*In [[Noragami]], Yato-no-kami appears briefly when their name is confused for that of the series protagonist, Yato or "Yatogami".<ref>Adachitoka. ''The Ties That Bind''. New York, NY: Kodansha USA Publishing, LLC, 2015. Print. Vol. 7 of ''Noragami: Stray God''.</ref>

==External links== *[http://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/cpjr/kami/ito.html Evolution of the Concept of Kami] *[http://www1.atwiki.jp/occultfantasy/pages/76.html 夜刀の神(ヤトノカミ・ヤツノカミ)] *[http://www.stonedragonpress.com/minzoku_neo_shinto/topic_004_04_03_02.html 民俗ネオ神道 – minzoku NEO-shintô – araburu-kami]
[[Category:Japanese legendary creatures]] [[Category:Legendary serpents]]
{{Japan-myth-stub}}

{{short description|Snake deities in Japanese folklore}} {{Refimprove|date=November 2011}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yato-no-kami'''|夜刀の神||"gods of the night-sword"}} are snake [[deity|deities]] in Japanese folklore appearing in the ''[[Fudoki|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki]]''. They lived in [[Namegata]] county, in fields near the government office. As a snake, it was an {{nihongo|'''araburu-kami'''|荒ぶる神||"malevolent-kami"}} which are a malignant [[kami]] who bring affliction to human beings.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.stonedragonpress.com/minzoku_neo_shinto/topic_004_04_03_02.html|title=民俗ネオ神道 – minzoku NEO-shintô – araburu-kami|website=www.stonedragonpress.com}}</ref> Yato no kami were rumored to bring familial extermination on anyone who saw them.
It's told that Yato no Kami were killed and enshrined by a man named Yahazu no uji no Matachi during the time when [[Emperor Keitai]] was in reign. The Yato no kami vanished later when a man named Mibunomuroji Maro drove them away from disturbing him and his workers who were building a moat there.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/cpjr/kami/ito.html|title=Evolution of the Concept of Kami|website=www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>
==References== <references />
==External links== *[http://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/cpjr/kami/ito.html Evolution of the Concept of Kami] *[http://www1.atwiki.jp/occultfantasy/pages/76.html 夜刀の神(ヤトノカミ・ヤツノカミ)] *[http://www.stonedragonpress.com/minzoku_neo_shinto/topic_004_04_03_02.html 民俗ネオ神道 – minzoku NEO-shintô – araburu-kami]
[[Category:Japanese legendary creatures]] [[Category:Legendary serpents]]
{{Japan-myth-stub}}

revision.text:
{{short description|Snake deities in Japanese folklore}} {{Refimprove|date=November 2011}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yato-no-kami'''|夜刀の神||"gods of the night-sword"}} are snake [[deity|deities]] in Japanese folklore appearing in the ''[[Fudoki|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki]]''.
== Properties of the gods == They lived in [[Namegata]] county, in fields near the government office. As a snake, it was an {{nihongo|'''araburu-kami'''|荒ぶる神||"rough, aggressive kami"}} which are fierce [[kami]] who represent the ferocious side of nature, such as raging rivers and fierce storms - as well as natural disasters. While such things do bring suffering to people, a properly enshrined and worshipped Araburu Kami would also protect the people from these calamities. <ref>{{Cite web|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/bts/detail/?id=3661|title=Araburu kami 荒ぶる神 Kokugakuin University|website=d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref> Yato no kami were rumored to bring familial extermination on anyone who saw them.
== Legends == It's told that Yato no Kami were killed and enshrined by a man named Yahazu no uji no Matachi during the time when [[Emperor Keitai]] was in reign. The Yato no kami vanished later when a man named Mibunomuroji Maro drove them away from disturbing him and his workers who were building a moat there.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/cpjr/kami/ito.html|title=Evolution of the Concept of Kami|website=www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>
==References== <references />
==External links== *[http://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/cpjr/kami/ito.html Evolution of the Concept of Kami] *[http://www1.atwiki.jp/occultfantasy/pages/76.html 夜刀の神(ヤトノカミ・ヤツノカミ)] *[https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/bts/detail/?id=3661 Araburu kami 荒ぶる神 Kokugakuin University]
[[Category:Japanese legendary creatures]] [[Category:Mythological and legendary Japanese snakes]] {{Japan-myth-stub}}

While such things do bring suffering to people, a properly enshrined and worshipped Araburu Kami would also protect the people from these calamities. <ref>{{Cite web|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/bts/detail/?id=3661|title=Araburu kami 荒ぶる神 Kokugakuin University|website=d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref> Yato no kami were rumored to bring familial extermination on anyone who saw them.
== Legends == It's told that Yato no Kami were killed and enshrined by a man named Yahazu no uji no Matachi during the time when [[Emperor Keitai]] was in reign. The Yato no kami vanished later when a man named Mibunomuroji Maro drove them away from disturbing him and his workers who were building a moat there.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/cpjr/kami/ito.html|title=Evolution of the Concept of Kami|website=www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>

While such things do bring suffering to people, a properly enshrined and worshipped Araburu Kami would also protect the people from these calamities. <ref>{{Cite web|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/bts/detail/?id=3661|title=Araburu kami 荒ぶる神 Kokugakuin University|website=d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref> Yato no kami were rumored to bring familial extermination on anyone who saw them.
== Legends == It's told that Yato no Kami were killed and enshrined by a man named Yahazu no uji no Matachi during the time when [[Emperor Keitai]] was in reign. The Yato no kami vanished later when a man named Mibunomuroji Maro drove them away from disturbing him and his workers who were building a moat there.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/cpjr/kami/ito.html|title=Evolution of the Concept of Kami|website=www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>
==References== <references />

{{short description|Snake deities in Japanese folklore}} {{Refimprove|date=November 2011}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yato-no-kami'''|夜刀の神||"gods of the night-sword"}} are snake [[deity|deities]] in Japanese folklore appearing in the ''[[Fudoki|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki]]''.
== Properties of the gods == They lived in [[Namegata]] county, in fields near the government office. As a snake, it was an {{nihongo|'''araburu-kami'''|荒ぶる神||"rough, aggressive kami"}} which are fierce [[kami]] who represent the ferocious side of nature, such as raging rivers and fierce storms - as well as natural disasters. While such things do bring suffering to people, a properly enshrined and worshipped Araburu Kami would also protect the people from these calamities. <ref>{{Cite web|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/bts/detail/?id=3661|title=Araburu kami 荒ぶる神 Kokugakuin University|website=d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref> Yato no kami were rumored to bring familial extermination on anyone who saw them.
== Legends == It's told that Yato no Kami were killed and enshrined by a man named Yahazu no uji no Matachi during the time when [[Emperor Keitai]] was in reign. The Yato no kami vanished later when a man named Mibunomuroji Maro drove them away from disturbing him and his workers who were building a moat there.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/cpjr/kami/ito.html|title=Evolution of the Concept of Kami|website=www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>

{{short description|Snake deities in Japanese folklore}} {{Refimprove|date=November 2011}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yato-no-kami'''|夜刀の神||"gods of the night-sword"}} are snake [[deity|deities]] in Japanese folklore appearing in the ''[[Fudoki|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki]]''.
== Properties of the gods == They lived in [[Namegata]] county, in fields near the government office. As a snake, it was an {{nihongo|'''araburu-kami'''|荒ぶる神||"rough, aggressive kami"}} which are fierce [[kami]] who represent the ferocious side of nature, such as raging rivers and fierce storms - as well as natural disasters. While such things do bring suffering to people, a properly enshrined and worshipped Araburu Kami would also protect the people from these calamities. <ref>{{Cite web|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/bts/detail/?id=3661|title=Araburu kami 荒ぶる神 Kokugakuin University|website=d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref> Yato no kami were rumored to bring familial extermination on anyone who saw them.
== Legends == It's told that Yato no Kami were killed and enshrined by a man named Yahazu no uji no Matachi during the time when [[Emperor Keitai]] was in reign. The Yato no kami vanished later when a man named Mibunomuroji Maro drove them away from disturbing him and his workers who were building a moat there.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/cpjr/kami/ito.html|title=Evolution of the Concept of Kami|website=www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>

{{short description|Snake deities in Japanese folklore}} {{Refimprove|date=November 2011}}

== Properties of the gods == They lived in [[Namegata]] county, in fields near the government office. == Legends == It's told that Yato no Kami were killed and enshrined by a man named Yahazu no uji no Matachi during the time when [[Emperor Keitai]] was in reign. The Yato no kami vanished later when a man named Mibunomuroji Maro drove them away from disturbing him and his workers who were building a moat there.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/cpjr/kami/ito.html|title=Evolution of the Concept of Kami|website=www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>

While such things do bring suffering to people, a properly enshrined and worshipped Araburu Kami would also protect the people from these calamities. <ref>{{Cite web|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/bts/detail/?id=3661|title=Araburu kami 荒ぶる神 Kokugakuin University|website=d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref> Yato no kami were rumored to bring familial extermination on anyone who saw them.
== Legends == It's told that Yato no Kami were killed and enshrined by a man named Yahazu no uji no Matachi during the time when [[Emperor Keitai]] was in reign. The Yato no kami vanished later when a man named Mibunomuroji Maro drove them away from disturbing him and his workers who were building a moat there.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/cpjr/kami/ito.html|title=Evolution of the Concept of Kami|website=www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>

{{short description|Snake deities in Japanese folklore}} {{Refimprove|date=November 2011}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yato-no-kami'''|夜刀の神||"gods of the night-sword"}} are snake [[deity|deities]] in Japanese folklore appearing in the ''[[Fudoki|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki]]''.
== Properties of the gods == They lived in [[Namegata]] county, in fields near the government office. As a snake, it was an {{nihongo|'''araburu-kami'''|荒ぶる神||"rough, aggressive kami"}} which are fierce [[kami]] who represent the ferocious side of nature, such as raging rivers and fierce storms - as well as natural disasters. While such things do bring suffering to people, a properly enshrined and worshipped Araburu Kami would also protect the people from these calamities. <ref>{{Cite web|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/bts/detail/?id=3661|title=Araburu kami 荒ぶる神 Kokugakuin University|website=d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref> Yato no kami were rumored to bring familial extermination on anyone who saw them.
== Legends == It's told that Yato no Kami were killed and enshrined by a man named Yahazu no uji no Matachi during the time when [[Emperor Keitai]] was in reign. The Yato no kami vanished later when a man named Mibunomuroji Maro drove them away from disturbing him and his workers who were building a moat there.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/cpjr/kami/ito.html|title=Evolution of the Concept of Kami|website=www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>
==References== <references />
==External links== *[http://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/cpjr/kami/ito.html Evolution of the Concept of Kami] *[http://www1.atwiki.jp/occultfantasy/pages/76.html 夜刀の神(ヤトノカミ・ヤツノカミ)] *[https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/bts/detail/?id=3661 Araburu kami 荒ぶる神 Kokugakuin University]
{{Japan-myth-stub}}

{{short description|Snake deities in Japanese folklore}} {{Refimprove|date=November 2011}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yato-no-kami'''|夜刀の神||"gods of the night-sword"}} are snake [[deity|deities]] in Japanese folklore appearing in the ''[[Fudoki|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki]]''.
== Properties of the gods == They lived in [[Namegata]] county, in fields near the government office. As a snake, it was an {{nihongo|'''araburu-kami'''|荒ぶる神||"rough, aggressive kami"}} which are fierce [[kami]] who represent the ferocious side of nature, such as raging rivers and fierce storms - as well as natural disasters. While such things do bring suffering to people, a properly enshrined and worshipped Araburu Kami would also protect the people from these calamities. <ref>{{Cite web|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/bts/detail/?id=3661|title=Araburu kami 荒ぶる神 Kokugakuin University|website=d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref> Yato no kami were rumored to bring familial extermination on anyone who saw them.
== Legends == It's told that Yato no Kami were killed and enshrined by a man named Yahazu no uji no Matachi during the time when [[Emperor Keitai]] was in reign. The Yato no kami vanished later when a man named Mibunomuroji Maro drove them away from disturbing him and his workers who were building a moat there.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/cpjr/kami/ito.html|title=Evolution of the Concept of Kami|website=www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>
==References== <references />
==External links== *[http://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/cpjr/kami/ito.html Evolution of the Concept of Kami] *[http://www1.atwiki.jp/occultfantasy/pages/76.html 夜刀の神(ヤトノカミ・ヤツノカミ)] *[https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/bts/detail/?id=3661 Araburu kami 荒ぶる神 Kokugakuin University] {{Japan-myth-stub}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Yato-no-kami]][[ja:夜刀神]][[zh:夜刀神]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q6145889]] Wikidata name: Yato-no-kami [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{short description|Snake deities in Japanese folklore}} {{Refimprove|date=November 2011}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yato-no-kami'''|夜刀の神||"gods of the night-sword"}} are snake [[Kami|deities]] in Japanese folklore appearing in the ''[[Fudoki|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki]]''.
== Properties of the gods == They lived in [[:en:Namegata|Namegata]] county, in fields near the government office. As a snake, it was an {{nihongo|'''araburu-kami'''|荒ぶる神||"rough, aggressive kami"}} which are fierce [[kami]] who represent the ferocious side of nature, such as raging rivers and fierce storms - as well as natural disasters. While such things do bring suffering to people, a properly enshrined and worshipped Araburu Kami would also protect the people from these calamities. <ref>{{Cite web|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/bts/detail/?id=3661|title=Araburu kami 荒ぶる神 Kokugakuin University|website=d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref> Yato no kami were rumored to bring familial extermination on anyone who saw them.
== Legends == It's told that Yato no Kami were killed and enshrined by a man named Yahazu no uji no Matachi during the time when [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]] was in reign. The Yato no kami vanished later when a man named Mibunomuroji Maro drove them away from disturbing him and his workers who were building a moat there.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/cpjr/kami/ito.html|title=Evolution of the Concept of Kami|website=www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>
==References== <references />
==External links== *[http://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/cpjr/kami/ito.html Evolution of the Concept of Kami] *[http://www1.atwiki.jp/occultfantasy/pages/76.html 夜刀の神(ヤトノカミ・ヤツノカミ)] *[https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/bts/detail/?id=3661 Araburu kami 荒ぶる神 Kokugakuin University] {{Japan-myth-stub}}
{{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese legendary creatures]] [[Category:Mythological and legendary Japanese snakes]]
}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Yato-no-kami]][[ja:夜刀神]][[zh:夜刀神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Yato-no-kami]][[ja:夜刀神]][[zh:夜刀神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Yato-no-kami]][[ja:夜刀神]][[zh:夜刀神]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q6145889]] Wikidata name: Yato-no-kami [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{short description|Snake deities in Japanese folklore}} {{Refimprove|date=November 2011}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yato-no-kami'''|夜刀の神||"gods of the night-sword"}} are snake [[Kami|deities]] in Japanese folklore appearing in the ''[[Fudoki|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki]]''.
== Properties of the gods == They lived in [[:en:Namegata|Namegata]] county, in fields near the government office. As a snake, it was an {{nihongo|'''araburu-kami'''|荒ぶる神||"rough, aggressive kami"}} which are fierce [[kami]] who represent the ferocious side of nature, such as raging rivers and fierce storms - as well as natural disasters. While such things do bring suffering to people, a properly enshrined and worshipped Araburu Kami would also protect the people from these calamities. <ref>{{Cite web|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/bts/detail/?id=3661|title=Araburu kami 荒ぶる神 Kokugakuin University|website=d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref> Yato no kami were rumored to bring familial extermination on anyone who saw them.
== Legends == It's told that Yato no Kami were killed and enshrined by a man named Yahazu no uji no Matachi during the time when [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]] was in reign. The Yato no kami vanished later when a man named Mibunomuroji Maro drove them away from disturbing him and his workers who were building a moat there.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/cpjr/kami/ito.html|title=Evolution of the Concept of Kami|website=www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>
==References== <references />
==External links== *[http://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/cpjr/kami/ito.html Evolution of the Concept of Kami] *[http://www1.atwiki.jp/occultfantasy/pages/76.html 夜刀の神(ヤトノカミ・ヤツノカミ)] *[https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/bts/detail/?id=3661 Araburu kami 荒ぶる神 Kokugakuin University] {{Japan-myth-stub}}
{{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese legendary creatures]] [[Category:Mythological and legendary Japanese snakes]]
}}

revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Yato-no-kami]][[ja:夜刀神]][[zh:夜刀神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Yato-no-kami]][[ja:夜刀神]][[zh:夜刀神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Yato-no-kami]][[ja:夜刀神]][[zh:夜刀神]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q6145889]] Wikidata name: Yato-no-kami [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{short description|Snake deities in Japanese folklore}} {{Refimprove|date=November 2011}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yato-no-kami'''|夜刀の神||"gods of the night-sword"}} are snake [[Kami|deities]] in Japanese folklore appearing in the ''[[Fudoki|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki]]''.
== Properties of the gods == They lived in {{ill|Namegata|en|Namegata|WD=Q877184|lt=Namegata}} county, in fields near the government office. As a snake, it was an {{nihongo|'''araburu-kami'''|荒ぶる神||"rough, aggressive kami"}} which are fierce [[kami]] who represent the ferocious side of nature, such as raging rivers and fierce storms - as well as natural disasters. While such things do bring suffering to people, a properly enshrined and worshipped Araburu Kami would also protect the people from these calamities. <ref>{{Cite web|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/bts/detail/?id=3661|title=Araburu kami 荒ぶる神 Kokugakuin University|website=d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref> Yato no kami were rumored to bring familial extermination on anyone who saw them.
== Legends == It's told that Yato no Kami were killed and enshrined by a man named Yahazu no uji no Matachi during the time when {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|WD=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai}} was in reign. The Yato no kami vanished later when a man named Mibunomuroji Maro drove them away from disturbing him and his workers who were building a moat there.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/cpjr/kami/ito.html|title=Evolution of the Concept of Kami|website=www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>
==References== <references />
==External links== *[http://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/cpjr/kami/ito.html Evolution of the Concept of Kami] *[http://www1.atwiki.jp/occultfantasy/pages/76.html 夜刀の神(ヤトノカミ・ヤツノカミ)] *[https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/bts/detail/?id=3661 Araburu kami 荒ぶる神 Kokugakuin University] {{Japan-myth-stub}}
[[en:Yato-no-kami]]
<!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[ja:夜刀神]] [[zh:夜刀神]]

The {{nihongo|'''Yato-no-kami'''|夜刀の神||"gods of the night-sword"}} are snake [[Kami|deities]] in Japanese folklore appearing in the ''[[Fudoki|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki]]''.
== Properties of the gods == They lived in {{ill|Namegata|en|Namegata|WD=Q877184|lt=Namegata|1=Namegata}} county, in fields near the government office. As a snake, it was an {{nihongo|'''araburu-kami'''|荒ぶる神||"rough, aggressive kami"}} which are fierce [[kami]] who represent the ferocious side of nature, such as raging rivers and fierce storms - as well as natural disasters. While such things do bring suffering to people, a properly enshrined and worshipped Araburu Kami would also protect the people from these calamities. <ref>{{Cite web|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/bts/detail/?id=3661|title=Araburu kami 荒ぶる神 Kokugakuin University|website=d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref> Yato no kami were rumored to bring familial extermination on anyone who saw them.
== Legends == It's told that Yato no Kami were killed and enshrined by a man named Yahazu no uji no Matachi during the time when {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|WD=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai|1=Emperor Keitai}} was in reign. The Yato no kami vanished later when a man named Mibunomuroji Maro drove them away from disturbing him and his workers who were building a moat there.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/cpjr/kami/ito.html|title=Evolution of the Concept of Kami|website=www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>
==References== <references />
==External links== *[http://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/cpjr/kami/ito.html Evolution of the Concept of Kami] *[http://www1.atwiki.jp/occultfantasy/pages/76.html 夜刀の神(ヤトノカミ・ヤツノカミ)] *[https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/bts/detail/?id=3661 Araburu kami 荒ぶる神 Kokugakuin University] {{Japan-myth-stub}}
